prescribing_info.title: BOTOX (onabotulinumtoxinA) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
BOTOX (onabotulinumtoxinA) FOR INJECTION - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Allergan (AbbVie)

BLACK BOX WARNING:
The effects of BOTOX and all botulinum toxin products may spread from the area of injection to produce symptoms consistent with botulinum toxin effects. These symptoms have been reported hours to weeks after injection. Swallowing and breathing difficulties can be life threatening.

INDICATIONS AND USAGE:
- Glabellar Lines: Temporary improvement of moderate to severe glabellar lines
- Forehead Lines: Temporary improvement of moderate to severe forehead lines
- Lateral Canthal Lines (Crow's Feet): Temporary improvement

DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION:

Glabellar Lines:
- Recommended dose: 20 Units total (4 Units per injection site x 5 sites)
- Injection sites: 2 in each corrugator muscle, 1 in procerus muscle
- MAXIMUM per treatment: 20 Units for glabellar lines alone

Forehead Lines:
- Recommended dose: 20 Units total (4 Units per injection site x 5 sites)
- Must treat glabellar lines concurrently to avoid brow ptosis
- Combined glabellar + forehead: 40 Units maximum

Lateral Canthal Lines (Crow's Feet):
- Recommended dose: 24 Units total (12 Units per side)
- 3 injection sites per side, 4 Units each
- Combined with glabellar: 44 Units total
- Combined with glabellar + forehead: 64 Units total

MAXIMUM RECOMMENDED DOSES:
- Glabellar alone: 20 Units
- Forehead alone: NOT recommended (treat with glabellar)
- Glabellar + Forehead: 40 Units
- Glabellar + Crow's Feet: 44 Units
- Glabellar + Forehead + Crow's Feet: 64 Units
- Maximum cumulative dose in 3-month period: 360 Units (all indications)

RECONSTITUTION:
- Use preservative-free 0.9% Sodium Chloride
- Recommended dilution: 2.5 mL saline per 100 Unit vial = 4 Units/0.1 mL
- Alternative: 1.25 mL per 100 Units = 8 Units/0.1 mL
- Alternative: 2.0 mL per 100 Units = 5 Units/0.1 mL
- Use within 24 hours of reconstitution (refrigerated)

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Infection at proposed injection site
- Known hypersensitivity to botulinum toxin or formulation components
- Hypersensitivity to human albumin

WARNINGS AND PRECAUTIONS:
- Spread of toxin effect: Dysphagia, breathing difficulties
- Pre-existing neuromuscular disorders: Increased risk of severe symptoms
- Dysphagia and breathing difficulties
- Corneal exposure and ulceration with periocular injections
- Human albumin: Remote risk of viral transmission

ADVERSE REACTIONS (≥1%):
- Eyelid ptosis (5.4%)
- Facial paresis (0.6%)
- Injection site reactions
- Headache

DRUG INTERACTIONS:
- Aminoglycosides: May potentiate effect
- Other botulinum toxin products: Avoid concurrent use
- Anticholinergics: May potentiate systemic anticholinergic effects
- Muscle relaxants: May exaggerate neuromuscular effects

USE IN SPECIFIC POPULATIONS:
- Pregnancy: No adequate studies; use only if clearly needed
- Lactation: Caution advised
- Pediatric: Safety not established for cosmetic use <18 years

STORAGE:
- Unopened: 2-8°C (36-46°F)
- Reconstituted: Use within 24 hours, refrigerated
- Do not freeze

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 50 Units per vial
- 100 Units per vial
- 200 Units per vial

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: BLA 103000
Label Revision: 01/2024
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=619f3e7c-7a19-4e28-a6d9-4c4b28e4e3c2

prescribing_info.title: DYSPORT (abobotulinumtoxinA) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
DYSPORT (abobotulinumtoxinA) FOR INJECTION - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Galderma

BLACK BOX WARNING:
The effects of DYSPORT and all botulinum toxin products may spread from the area of injection. Swallowing and breathing difficulties can be life threatening.

UNIT CONVERSION NOTE:
Dysport units are NOT interchangeable with other botulinum toxin products. Conversion ratio approximately 2.5-3:1 (Dysport:Botox)

INDICATIONS:
- Glabellar Lines: Temporary improvement of moderate to severe glabellar lines

DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION:

Glabellar Lines:
- Recommended dose: 50 Units total
- 5 injection sites: 10 Units per site
- 2 injections in each corrugator, 1 in procerus
- Inject at least 1 cm above brow

MAXIMUM RECOMMENDED DOSE:
- Glabellar lines: 50 Units per treatment session
- Minimum interval: 3 months between treatments
- Do not exceed 50 Units in glabellar region

RECONSTITUTION:
- 300 Unit vial: Add 1.5 mL, 2.5 mL, or other as per chart
- 2.5 mL reconstitution = 10 Units/0.08 mL
- 1.5 mL reconstitution = 10 Units/0.05 mL

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Hypersensitivity to formulation
- Cow's milk protein allergy (contains lactose from cow's milk)
- Infection at injection site

WARNINGS:
- Contains albumin: Theoretical viral transmission risk
- Spread of toxin effect
- Dysphagia (more common with cervical dystonia)
- Pre-existing neuromuscular conditions

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Nasopharyngitis (7%)
- Headache (6%)
- Injection site pain (5%)
- Injection site reaction (2%)
- Upper respiratory infection (2%)
- Eyelid edema (2%)
- Eyelid ptosis (2%)
- Sinusitis (1%)

STORAGE:
- Refrigerate 2-8°C
- Use within 24 hours of reconstitution
- Protect from light

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 300 Units per vial
- 500 Units per vial

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: BLA 125274
Label Revision: 09/2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=61e8d2a7-6e8d-48f7-9b5c-3e1c56e9f65a

prescribing_info.title: XEOMIN (incobotulinumtoxinA) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
XEOMIN (incobotulinumtoxinA) FOR INJECTION - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Merz Pharmaceuticals

BLACK BOX WARNING:
Same as other botulinum toxins - distant spread of toxin effect possible.

PRODUCT CHARACTERISTICS:
- Highly purified neurotoxin without complexing proteins
- May reduce immunogenicity
- Units NOT interchangeable with other products
- Approximate 1:1 ratio with Botox in clinical studies

INDICATIONS:
- Glabellar Lines: Moderate to severe in adults
- Forehead Lines: In conjunction with glabellar treatment
- Lateral Canthal Lines

DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION:

Glabellar Lines:
- Recommended dose: 20 Units total
- 5 injection sites, 4 Units each
- Same pattern as BOTOX

Forehead Lines:
- Recommended dose: 10 Units total
- 5 injection sites, 2 Units each (when given with glabellar)
- Combined: 30 Units total

Lateral Canthal Lines:
- 12 Units per side (24 Units total)
- 3 sites per side, 4 Units each

MAXIMUM DOSES:
- Glabellar alone: 20 Units
- Glabellar + Forehead: 30 Units
- Glabellar + Crow's Feet: 44 Units
- All three areas: 54 Units

RECONSTITUTION:
- Use 0.9% preservative-free saline
- 1.25 mL per 50 Units = 4 Units/0.1 mL
- 2.5 mL per 100 Units = 4 Units/0.1 mL
- Room temperature storage after reconstitution acceptable for 24 hours

ADVANTAGES:
- No complexing proteins (reduced immunogenicity potential)
- Can be stored at room temperature prior to reconstitution

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Hypersensitivity to botulinum toxin
- Infection at injection site

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Headache (5%)
- Facial paresis (4%)
- Injection site pain
- Nasopharyngitis

STORAGE:
- Unopened: Room temperature (up to 25°C) or refrigerated
- After reconstitution: Use within 24 hours, refrigerated

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 50 Units per vial
- 100 Units per vial

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: BLA 125360
Label Revision: 12/2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=ccda8a27-4361-485c-9c35-78b80fe0e1b0

prescribing_info.title: JUVÉDERM Family Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
JUVÉDERM COLLECTION OF FILLERS - PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Allergan (AbbVie)

PRODUCT OVERVIEW:
All JUVÉDERM products are hyaluronic acid (HA) dermal fillers with VYCROSS or Hylacross technology.

JUVÉDERM VOLUMA XC:
- Indication: Cheek augmentation, age-related volume loss in midface
- HA concentration: 20 mg/mL
- Lidocaine: 0.3%
- Maximum volume: 20 mL per cheek, 6 mL per treatment session recommended
- Injection depth: Subcutaneous and/or supraperiosteal
- Duration: Up to 24 months

JUVÉDERM VOLLURE XC:
- Indication: Moderate to severe nasolabial folds
- HA concentration: 17.5 mg/mL
- Maximum: 6 mL per treatment recommended
- Injection depth: Mid to deep dermis
- Duration: Up to 18 months

JUVÉDERM VOLBELLA XC:
- Indication: Lip augmentation, perioral rhytids
- HA concentration: 15 mg/mL
- Maximum lips: 3 mL per treatment
- Maximum perioral: 3 mL per treatment
- Injection depth: Superficial dermis to deep dermis/submucosal
- Duration: Up to 12 months

JUVÉDERM Ultra XC:
- Indication: Lip augmentation, moderate to severe facial wrinkles
- HA concentration: 24 mg/mL
- Maximum: 6 mL total; lips 1-2 mL per session recommended
- Duration: Up to 12 months

JUVÉDERM Ultra Plus XC:
- Indication: Moderate to severe nasolabial folds
- HA concentration: 24 mg/mL
- More robust gel than Ultra XC
- Maximum: 6 mL per treatment
- Duration: Up to 12 months

JUVÉDERM VOLUX XC:
- Indication: Jawline definition and chin augmentation
- HA concentration: 25 mg/mL
- Firmest JUVÉDERM product
- Maximum: 6 mL per treatment
- Injection: Deep subcutaneous/supraperiosteal
- Duration: Up to 18 months

CONTRAINDICATIONS (ALL PRODUCTS):
- Severe allergies with history of anaphylaxis
- History of multiple severe allergies
- Patients with bleeding disorders
- Patients on anticoagulants (relative)
- Known hypersensitivity to hyaluronic acid or lidocaine
- Active skin infection or inflammation at injection site

WARNINGS AND PRECAUTIONS:
- Intravascular injection: Can cause vision abnormalities, blindness, stroke, skin necrosis
- Avoid injection in or near blood vessels
- Use appropriate injection technique
- History of herpes: May trigger reactivation
- Immunocompromised patients: Use with caution
- Inflammatory skin conditions: May reduce duration

VASCULAR OCCLUSION SIGNS:
- Blanching, dusky discoloration
- Pain disproportionate to injection
- Vision changes
- Necrosis
IMMEDIATE TREATMENT: Hyaluronidase

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Injection site swelling (90%+)
- Injection site tenderness
- Bruising (40-60%)
- Pain, erythema
- Nodules/lumps (1-5%)
- Rare: Granulomas, vascular occlusion

HYALURONIDASE REVERSAL:
- Recommended initial dose: 150-300 Units
- May require additional doses
- Massage area after injection
- Check results in 24-48 hours

STORAGE:
- Room temperature (up to 30°C)
- Protect from freezing and sunlight
- Use by expiration date on package

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: Various PMAs
Label Revision: 2024
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/search.cfm?labeltype=all&query=juvederm

prescribing_info.title: RESTYLANE Family Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
RESTYLANE COLLECTION OF FILLERS - PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Galderma

PRODUCT OVERVIEW:
NASHA (Non-Animal Stabilized Hyaluronic Acid) technology and XpresHAn technology dermal fillers.

RESTYLANE:
- Indication: Moderate to severe facial wrinkles and folds
- HA concentration: 20 mg/mL
- Gel particle size: 250-350 μm
- Maximum per treatment: 6.8 mL
- Duration: 6-12 months

RESTYLANE-L:
- Same as Restylane with 0.3% lidocaine
- Maximum: 6.8 mL per treatment

RESTYLANE LYFT:
- Indication: Moderate to severe facial folds, cheek augmentation, correction of age-related midface volume deficit, dorsal hand rejuvenation
- HA concentration: 20 mg/mL
- Larger particles for lifting capacity
- Maximum cheeks: 6 mL per treatment
- Maximum hands: 3 mL per hand per treatment
- Duration: Up to 12 months (face), 6 months (hands)

RESTYLANE SILK:
- Indication: Lip augmentation, perioral rhytids
- HA concentration: 20 mg/mL
- Smallest particles in Restylane family
- Maximum lips: 1.5 mL per treatment recommended
- Duration: Up to 6 months

RESTYLANE KYSSE:
- Indication: Lip augmentation and perioral rhytids
- XpresHAn technology for natural movement
- Maximum lips: 1.5 mL recommended (up to 3 mL if needed)
- Duration: Up to 12 months

RESTYLANE REFYNE:
- Indication: Moderate to severe facial wrinkles and folds
- XpresHAn technology - flexible gel
- Maximum: 6 mL per treatment
- For natural movement areas (smile lines)
- Duration: Up to 12 months

RESTYLANE DEFYNE:
- Indication: Moderate to severe deep facial wrinkles and folds
- XpresHAn technology - supportive gel
- More cohesive than Refyne
- Maximum: 6 mL per treatment
- Duration: Up to 12 months

RESTYLANE CONTOUR:
- Indication: Cheek augmentation and midface contouring
- XpresHAn technology
- Maximum: 6 mL per treatment (3 mL per cheek)
- Duration: Up to 12 months

RESTYLANE EYELIGHT:
- Indication: Infraorbital hollow correction
- Specifically designed for under-eye area
- Maximum: 1.5 mL per treatment (both eyes)
- Superficial injection technique required

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Severe allergies
- History of anaphylaxis
- Hypersensitivity to gram-positive bacterial proteins
- Lidocaine allergy (for products containing lidocaine)

WARNINGS:
- Intravascular injection risk
- Implantation too superficially may result in visible product
- Avoid in patients with active inflammatory conditions
- Herpes reactivation risk
- Not for breast augmentation

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Swelling, redness, pain, bruising, headache
- Tenderness, lump/bump, itching
- Typically resolve within 7 days

STORAGE:
- Store at room temperature 2-25°C
- Do not freeze
- Use before expiration date

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: Various PMAs
Label Revision: 2024
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/search.cfm?labeltype=all&query=restylane

prescribing_info.title: KYBELLA (deoxycholic acid) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
KYBELLA (deoxycholic acid) INJECTION - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Allergan (AbbVie)

INDICATION:
Improvement in the appearance of moderate to severe convexity or fullness associated with submental fat in adults.

MECHANISM:
Deoxycholic acid is a cytolytic drug that causes adipocyte cell membrane disruption, resulting in adipocyte lysis. Following adipocytolysis, lipids are cleared through lipid homeostasis and metabolism.

DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION:

Injection Technique:
- Area of submental fat marked with grid
- Injections spaced 1 cm apart
- Inject 0.2 mL per injection site

DOSING LIMITS:
- Maximum per injection site: 0.2 mL
- Maximum injection sites per treatment: 50 sites
- Maximum volume per treatment: 10 mL (50 sites × 0.2 mL)
- Maximum treatments: Up to 6 treatments
- Minimum interval between treatments: 1 month
- Maximum cumulative dose: 100 mg (10 mL per session)

NUMBER OF TREATMENTS:
- Average 2-4 treatment sessions for optimal results
- Re-treatment depends on amount of submental fat and treatment goals
- 59% of patients achieved response at 12 weeks with 2+ treatments

PREPARATION:
- Supplied as 2 mL vials (10 mg/mL)
- Each vial contains 20 mg deoxycholic acid
- Do not dilute
- Inject at room temperature

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Infection at injection site

WARNINGS AND PRECAUTIONS:

Marginal Mandibular Nerve Injury:
- Can cause asymmetric smile, facial weakness
- Reported in 4% of clinical trial subjects
- Usually resolves spontaneously (median 44 days)
- Avoid injecting near nerve

Dysphagia:
- Reported in 2% of subjects
- Usually resolves within days to weeks
- Use caution in patients with prior difficulty swallowing

Injection Site Alopecia:
- Hair loss at treatment area reported
- May persist after treatment

Skin Ulceration and Necrosis:
- Reported with use
- Do not inject into or around other anatomic structures

AVOID TREATMENT IN:
- Presence of infection in treatment area
- Patients with difficulty swallowing
- Excessive skin laxity (may worsen appearance)
- Prior surgical or aesthetic treatment to submental area

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Injection site edema/swelling (87%)
- Injection site hematoma/bruising (72%)
- Injection site pain (70%)
- Injection site numbness (66%)
- Injection site erythema (27%)
- Injection site induration (22%)
- Injection site paresthesia (14%)
- Headache (8%)
- Oropharyngeal pain (3%)

POST-TREATMENT:
- Significant swelling expected (1-2 weeks)
- Numbness may persist 1-2 months
- Ice application recommended
- No strenuous activity for 24-48 hours

STORAGE:
- Refrigerate 2-8°C (36-46°F)
- Do not freeze
- Protect from light
- May store at room temperature up to 77°F for up to 48 hours prior to use

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 2 mL single-patient-use vial (10 mg/mL = 20 mg per vial)
- Carton of 4 vials

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: NDA 206333
Label Revision: 06/2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=f2aaa883-6c5f-4b46-aac6-ebe00e5e6f25

prescribing_info.title: SCULPTRA Aesthetic Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
SCULPTRA AESTHETIC (INJECTABLE POLY-L-LACTIC ACID) - PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Galderma

INDICATION:
Correction of shallow to deep nasolabial fold contour deficiencies and other facial wrinkles. Indicated for healthy people who desire correction of facial fat loss.

MECHANISM:
Poly-L-lactic acid (PLLA) is a biocompatible, biodegradable synthetic polymer. It stimulates collagen production through a gradual, natural-looking correction process.

PRODUCT COMPOSITION:
- PLLA: 150 mg per vial
- Sodium carboxymethylcellulose (CMC): 90 mg
- Non-pyrogenic mannitol: 127.5 mg
- Sterile, lyophilized powder

RECONSTITUTION:
- Minimum reconstitution volume: 5 mL sterile water for injection
- Recommended: 7-8 mL for facial use
- Reconstitute at least 2 hours before use (24-72 hours preferred)
- Can store reconstituted product refrigerated up to 72 hours
- Agitate before use

DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION:

Injection Technique:
- Deep dermis or subcutaneous layer
- Cross-hatching or fanning technique
- Avoid superficial injection (visible nodules)
- Massage after injection (5-5-5 rule)

VOLUME GUIDELINES:
- Per injection site: 0.05-0.2 mL
- Maximum per cheek per session: 2.5-3 mL of reconstituted product
- Maximum total per session: Up to 8-10 mL (1-2 vials)

TREATMENT SCHEDULE:
- Average 3 treatment sessions
- Interval: 3-6 weeks between sessions
- Results develop gradually over 2-3 months
- Duration: Up to 25 months

TREATMENT AREAS:
- Temples (volume loss)
- Cheeks (midface volume)
- Nasolabial folds (deep injection)
- Marionette lines
- Pre-jowl sulcus
- Jawline
- Chin
NOT for lips or periorbital area

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Active skin infection or inflammation at injection site
- Known hypersensitivity to any component
- NOT for use in lips or periorbital area

WARNINGS:
- Nodule formation with superficial injection
- Not for use where permanent implants are present
- Delayed onset nodules possible (months later)
- Keloid or hypertrophic scarring history requires caution
- Immunocompromised patients - use with caution

POST-PROCEDURE MASSAGE:
- Critical for even distribution
- 5-5-5 Rule: Massage 5 times daily for 5 minutes for 5 days
- Reduces nodule risk

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Injection site swelling, redness, pain, bruising
- Small nodules (may be palpable, usually not visible)
- Papules (if injected too superficially)
- Rare: granulomas

NODULE MANAGEMENT:
- Subclinical nodules: Often resolve spontaneously
- Visible nodules: May require intralesional steroids
- Refractory: Excision may be required

STORAGE:
- Store at controlled room temperature 15-30°C
- Reconstituted: Refrigerate, use within 72 hours

HOW SUPPLIED:
- Single vial containing 150 mg PLLA
- Carton of 2 vials

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: PMA P030050
Label Revision: 2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=d15d7e6d-75d7-4a74-b9d7-3c6b4a1e9f8c

prescribing_info.title: RADIESSE Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
RADIESSE (CALCIUM HYDROXYLAPATITE) - PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Merz Aesthetics

PRODUCT DESCRIPTION:
Calcium hydroxylapatite (CaHA) microspheres (25-45 μm) suspended in carboxymethylcellulose gel carrier. Provides immediate volume and stimulates collagen production.

INDICATIONS:
- Correction of moderate to severe facial wrinkles and folds
- Volume loss in the back of hands
- HIV-associated facial lipoatrophy
- Subdermal implantation for correction of volume deficit

PRODUCT FORMS:
- RADIESSE: Standard formulation
- RADIESSE (+): Contains 0.3% lidocaine
- Available in 0.8 mL, 1.5 mL, and 3.0 mL syringes

COMPOSITION:
- CaHA microspheres: 30% by volume
- Gel carrier: 70% by volume
- Total CaHA: 0.3 mL per 1.0 mL of product

DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION:

Facial Wrinkles and Folds:
- Inject into deep dermis or subcutaneous tissue
- Linear threading, fanning, or cross-hatching technique
- Maximum per treatment: 3.0 mL per side of face
- Typical volume per session: 1.5-3.0 mL total

Hand Rejuvenation:
- 1.5 mL per hand per treatment
- Maximum: 3.0 mL per hand per treatment
- Dilute with lidocaine and saline (1:1:1 ratio)
- Inject subdermally with linear threading
- Massage immediately after injection

DILUTION FOR HANDS:
- 1.5 mL Radiesse + 0.75 mL lidocaine 2% + 0.75 mL saline
- Increases spreadability
- Improves patient comfort

HYPERDILUTION (off-label):
- For skin quality improvement
- 1.5 mL Radiesse + 2-3 mL saline
- Subdermal injection for collagen stimulation

DURATION:
- Immediate volumizing effect
- Collagen stimulation over 2-3 months
- Results lasting 12-18 months (face)
- 6-12 months (hands)

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Hypersensitivity to any component
- Active infection at injection site
- Bleeding disorders
- NOT for injection in lips
- NOT for superficial injection (visible granules)

WARNINGS:
- Not reversible (unlike HA fillers)
- Intravascular injection can cause necrosis, blindness
- Avoid in patients with history of keloids
- Avoid in lips - causes nodules
- May be visible on X-ray, CT, ultrasound

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Bruising, swelling, pain at injection site
- Redness, itching
- Nodules (especially if injected superficially)
- Rare: Granulomas, infection

Hands-Specific Reactions:
- Bruising more common
- Tenderness
- Temporary restricted motion

NOT REVERSIBLE:
- No enzymatic reversal agent (unlike hyaluronidase for HA)
- Nodules may require surgical excision
- Intralesional steroids may help reduce inflammation

STORAGE:
- Room temperature
- Do not freeze
- Use before expiration date

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 0.8 mL, 1.5 mL, 3.0 mL prefilled syringes
- RADIESSE (+) with lidocaine in same volumes

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: PMA P050037
Label Revision: 2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=1c2e3d4f-5a6b-7c8d-9e0f-1a2b3c4d5e6f
